speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: A goodly city is this Antium. City,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: ’Tis I that made thy widows. Many an heir

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Of these fair edifices ’fore my wars

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Have I heard groan and drop. Then, know me not,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Lest that thy wives with spits and boys with stones

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: In puny battle slay me.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Save you, sir.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: And you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Direct me, if it be your will,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Where great Aufidius lies. Is he in Antium?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: He is, and feasts the nobles of the state

speaker: CITIZEN
text: At his house this night.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Which is his house, beseech you?

speaker: CITIZEN
text: This here before you.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Thank you, sir. Farewell.

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: O world, thy slippery turns! Friends now fast sworn,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whose double bosoms seems to wear one heart,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whose hours, whose bed, whose meal and exercise

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Are still together, who twin, as ’twere, in love

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Unseparable, shall within this hour,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: On a dissension of a doit, break out

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To bitterest enmity; so fellest foes,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Whose passions and whose plots have broke their sleep

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: To take the one the other, by some chance,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: Some trick not worth an egg, shall grow dear friends

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: And interjoin their issues. So with me:

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: My birthplace hate I, and my love’s upon

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: This enemy town. I’ll enter. If he slay me,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: He does fair justice; if he give me way,

speaker: CORIOLANUS
text: I’ll do his country service.
